methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $400M last post money.

data.narrative:
AiDash is a Series C infrastructure and logistics company founded by Abhishek Singh that has raised $91M+ to date against a stated post-money valuation of $400M+. The valuation assessment is constrained by the absence of public comparables and limited availability of recent private comparable transactions in the infrastructure logistics sector.

Given the data available, a single valuation method—stage-progression analysis—is applicable. This approach applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier, typical for Series C to subsequent-stage advancement, to the $400M last post-money valuation. This calculation implies a forward valuation of $680M. The stage-progression framework reflects venture-backed capital progression patterns but does not incorporate sector-specific growth multiples or revenue metrics, as those inputs are not provided.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $578M to $782M, with a median point of $680M. This represents a 45% to 95% increase from the current $400M post-money. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable company analysis—no public or private comps are available to cross-check whether stage-typical multiples apply to infrastructure logistics specifically—and the lack of underlying financial or operational metrics. Additional diligence on revenue run-rate, unit economics, and peer performance would be necessary to validate or refine this range.
